candidate_problems.text: Can 'irreducible perspectivality' be made decomposition-invariant and non-arbitrary? The conjecture depends on claims like 'destroyed by every decomposition below the whole-system boundary,' but what counts as a legitimate part, module, interface, or message-passing factorization is underdetermined. A key open problem is to define a principled decomposition class and prove that the purported perspectival variables are not artifacts of modeling choice, coordinate choice, or computational resource limits of the observer.

Is there a rigorous criterion separating genuinely irreducible globally bound evaluative computation from merely complex but reducible integration? Many nonconscious systems (brains, controllers, deep networks, recurrent agents, markets) exhibit global state, feedback, and joint constraints. The unresolved tension is whether the conjecture identifies a novel computational property with clear necessary/sufficient conditions, or redescribes familiar integration/partial observability/control-theoretic phenomena. A worthwhile problem is to construct formal examples and counterexamples where predictive or control power provably collapses under decomposition in one case but not the other.

What is the explanatory bridge from irreducible perspectival computation to subjective experience rather than only distinctive control architecture? The conjecture sharpens a functional discriminator, but it remains open why a bounded first-person compression with self-regulatory significance should instantiate phenomenology at all. The central new problem is to determine whether irreducible perspectivality is merely correlated with consciousness, constitutive of it, or insufficient without additional properties such as specific temporal depth, valence structure, embodiment, or representational content.
